disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Continuous foot/toe pain at rest, present through the night, relieved only by hanging the foot down and worsened by elevation
trigger_question_examples: When does the pain come on — with walking, or even at rest?
Is there any position that makes it better or worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It never stops now, even lying in bed. The only thing that helps is hanging my foot off the side of the bed. Putting it up on a pillow makes it much worse.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New black, hard, dried-out tissue at the tip of the great toe (tissue loss)
trigger_question_examples: What does the toe look like?
Has the colour of any part of the foot changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The tip of my big toe has gone black and hard — it happened today and it looks like dead skin.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Non-healing punched-out ulcer on the lateral malleolus
trigger_question_examples: Are there any sores or wounds on the foot or leg?
Do you have any breaks in the skin that won't heal?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, there's a deep sore on the outer side of my ankle that's been there and won't close up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The affected foot feels perishingly cold and looks pale/shiny
trigger_question_examples: Does the foot feel cold compared with the other one?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's freezing cold compared to my right foot, and the skin looks pale and shiny.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Prior history of exertional calf cramping (claudication) that has rapidly escalated to continuous rest pain over two weeks
trigger_question_examples: Have you had leg pain with walking before this?
How has the pain changed over time?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: For a while I'd get a cramp in the calf when I walked and it would ease if I stopped. But in the last couple of weeks it's turned into this constant pain that never lets up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, rigors, and no signs of wet/infected tissue (toe not hot, swollen, oozing or malodorous)
trigger_question_examples: Is the black toe hot, swollen, weeping or smelly?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fevers or shivers. The toe's not hot or wet or smelly — it's just black and dry.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe unremitting pain causing days of sleep deprivation in a person living alone
trigger_question_examples: How is this affecting your sleep and daily life?
Is there anyone at home with you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own and I haven't slept properly in days — the pain is unbearable and I don't know how much more I can take.
